Samurai House · Nakasendo · Culture & Faith

The old roadremembers everything.

Masugata

The road that bends on purpose

A deliberate double turn in the post town — built to slow any advancing force.

Shukuba

The post towns of the Nakasendo

Resting stops with inns, meals, and fresh horses along the mountain highway.

Kōshin

The night no one slept

On certain nights people stayed awake till dawn, so the spirits within could not slip away.

Kitsune Kōyaku

The fox’s healing salve

A local Ochiai legend of a medicinal salve — tied to the Medicine Buddha enshrined at Iouji.

Maguchi Zei

Why the houses are long and narrow

Tax was charged by street frontage — so homes were built narrow and deep.

Mura-hazure no Hakaba

The cemetery at the village edge

Hillside graves watch over the rice fields, marked with the chant of refuge in Amida Buddha.

Sangaku Shinkō

Mountain worship

Seeking enlightenment and purification through the mountains themselves.

Chinju no Mori

The village guardian forest

Ancient cedars sheltering the local shrine — left uncut as the home of the deity.

Nakatsugawa · Gifu · Japan
